channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Heavy snow brings widespread disruption across the UK and Germany
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heavy snow and freezing rain brought widespread disruption across Europe on Sunday, particularly in the U.K. and Germany, with several major airports forced to suspend flights With the weather set to stay inclement on Sunday in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A major winter storm threatens parts of the central U.S. with snow, ice and even blizzards
item.description: A major, disruptive winter storm was sweeping across the central U.S. on Sunday, forecasters said, bringing with it a dreaded combination of snow, ice and plunging temperatures.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to watch the Golden Globes and red carpet fashions before the show
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes are returning for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NYC subway burning suspect, Laken Riley’s killer among illegal immigrants accused of preying on Americans
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta, who allegedly set a woman on fire in the NYC subway, and Laken Riley's killer, Jose Ibarra, were among violent migrants arrested or convicted in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drawn to New Orleans' iconic street of celebration, a night of partying becomes a nightmare
item.description: The night, like countless others Bourbon Street has welcomed over the decades, started out ripe for celebration.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Soldier who exploded Cybertruck in Las Vegas did not show any 'concerning behavior' before leave request: Army
item.description: The driver who exploded a Cybertruck in front of the Trump hotel in Las Vegas didn't show concerning behaviors at the time he was granted personal leave from his station, the Army said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: A British man who was killed when the driver of a white pickup truck sped down Bourbon Street packed with holiday revelers was identified Saturday as the stepson of a former nanny to the Royal Family. Prince William expressed shock and sadness at the news of the death of Edward Pettifer of London.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania man who served in Army indicted on charges he attempted to join Hezbollah 'to kill Jews': DOJ
item.description: A former member of the U.S. Army from Pennsylvania is facing federal charges after authorities say he traveled overseas to join the terrorist militant group, Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicago Bulls plan to retire Derrick Rose's jersey next season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Derrick Rose wasn't sure how he will feel when sees his No. 1 hanging from the rafters. He was still trying to process the news. The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday they plan to retire Rose's jersey sometime next season.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A cartoonist has decided to quit her job at the Washington Post after an editor rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing before President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indie filmmaker Jeff Baena, Aubrey Plaza's husband, found dead at Los Angeles home
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Writer-director Jeff Baena, whose darkly comedic independent films included “The Little Hours” and who was married to his frequent creative collaborator Aubrey Plaza, has died. He was 47.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 39,000 John Deere mowers recalled
item.description: About 39,000 John Deere ZTrak™ Zero Turn Mowers with Kawasaki Engines have been recalled because the voltage regulator inside the mower's engine can fail while in use or while the product is being stored.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs finally depart KC for Denver amid ice storm after 4 hours spent waiting on the tarmac
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Chiefs finally departed from Kansas City International Airport for their game in Denver on Saturday after spending about four hours stranded on the tarmac amid an ice storm that blanketed the region ahead of an impending blizzard.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs stranded on runway after KCI shuts down airfield
item.description: While the team was set to depart this afternoon, the weather, along with a broken-down treatment truck, forced KCI to shut down the airfield. Now, the FOX4 sports team has confirmed their plane is stuck on the runway.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Seckington, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WGN 9

item.title: Hundreds of animals killed in Dallas shopping center fire
item.description: A fire that broke out at a shopping center in Dallas on Friday morning killed more than 500 animals, most of which were small birds, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Filmmaker and husband to Aubrey Plaza, Jeff Baena, dies by suicide: report
item.description: Film writer and director Jeff Baena, who is also known for his marriage to Hollywood actress Aubrey Plaza, has died at the age of 47, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Florida mom makes teen son turn himself in for crime
item.description: A 15-year-old boy accused of armed attempted robbery was brought to Florida deputies to be turned in, by his own mother.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: Paleontologists learn from rare 'Teen Rex' specimen
item.description: Details about the juvenile Tyrannosaurus rex fossil discovered by three boys in North Dakota are beginning to take shape, including its size, age and how its body worked.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's Kelce! Retired Eagles great Jason Kelce tries his hand as a late-night television show host
item.description: Jason Kelce threw a late-night bash — and he brought his wife, his parents, a fan with a Phillie Phanatic tattoo inked on his navel, a Philly band and an uncensored (at least on stage) take on sports for the first of five episodes of “They Call It Late Night with Jason Kelce” on ESPN.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Orleans officers recovering after 'heroic' response to New Year's terrorist attack: attorney
item.description: Two New Orleans Police Department officers injured in the Jan. 1 terrorist attack on Bourbon Street that left 15 dead are expected to make a full recovery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2nd Homeland Security agent in Utah arrested on charges of selling illicit drugs that were seized as evidence
item.description: A second Department of Homeland Security agent in Utah was arrested after allegedly using a confidential informant to sell illicit drugs from evidence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sunday school class with Jimmy Carter: What it was like
item.description: PLAINS, Ga. (AP) — It never got old. No matter how many times one crammed into the modest sanctuary at Maranatha Baptist Church, there was always some wisdom to be gleaned from the measured, Bible-inspired words of Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: PAUL NEWBERRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Soldier who blew up Tesla at Trump hotel left note saying blast was to be a 'wake up call' for US
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A highly decorated Army soldier who fatally shot himself in a Tesla Cybertruck just before it blew up outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left notes saying the New Year's Day explosion was a stunt to serve as a “wake up call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thornton Township suspends senior bus program in latest service snag to residents
item.description: The service is the latest disruption to residents due to a lapse in insurance coverage. In December, Thornton Township Hall was abruptly closed, leaving residents unable to access some municipal services.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, NewsNation

item.title: Former employee charged with child abuse after babies found with 'unexplainable fractures' at Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s NICU
item.description: A former employee of Henrico Doctors' Hospital’s neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) has been charged with malicious wounding and child abuse after an investigation into babies being found with "unexplainable fractures" at the NICU.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, NewsNation

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star Zachery Ty Bryan jailed in Myrtle Beach
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan was arrested this week on a domestic violence charge in Myrtle Beach, online jail records show.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jill Biden got most expensive gift from a foreign leader in 2023. Here's what it was
item.description: President Joe Biden and his family were given tens of thousands of dollars in gifts from foreign leaders in 2023, according to an annual accounting published by the State Department on Thursday, with first lady Jill Biden receiving the single most expensive present: a $20,000 diamond from India’s le
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Johnson battles for the Speakership: 5 things to watch
item.description: Friday’s Speaker vote is shaping up to be a nail-biter as Rep. Mike Johnson (R-La.), the current House leader, is scrambling to keep the gavel for the next two years in the face of conservative critics furious with his leadership style.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: FBI releases new video of a suspect planting a pipe bomb near DNC offices on eve of the Capitol riot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol? Hoping to generate new tips from the public, the FBI is releasing
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Survivor of New Orleans terror attack recalls 'surreal' aftermath of deadly attack that shattered his legs
item.description: Jeremi Sensky was returning to his hotel after meeting friends in the early hours on New Year’s Day when he heard a "massive noise" — the last thing he remembers before ending up facedown on the ground with his wheelchair smashed around him.
item.dc:creator: Tom Llamas and Doha Madani, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Duchess Meghan Netflix series set for January premiere
item.description: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, has a Netflix series set for a mid-January premiere, the streaming service said in a post on the social media platform X on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: The FBI said it believes it's the largest amount of homemade explosives it has encountered in its history
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, WFLA, NBC
